items.title: Specificity and source order: reject the invalid model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling misconception? Specificity is one total number where ten classes always equal one id.
hints: Compare both options with platform semantics and observable user or browser evidence.

feedback.correct: Correct. Treat specificity as compared components, not base-ten arithmetic, and keep component selectors intentionally low.
feedback.incorrect: That preserves the misconception. Treat specificity as compared components, not base-ten arithmetic, and keep component selectors intentionally low.

criteria.description: Selects the source-linked CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling correction rather than the documented misconception.

options.text: Treat specificity as compared components, not base-ten arithmetic, and keep component selectors intentionally low.

items.title: Specificity and source order: verified implementation facet
items.prompt: Calculate selector specificity and prefer maintainable ordering over escalation. Build a bounded artifact demonstrating specificity then source order; the locked browser verifier checks observable structure, behavior, or computed presentation.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted authoring draft. Runtime evidence does not replace independent human standards, pedagogy, accessibility, answer-oracle, and formal-exam review.

hints: Implement the smallest artifact that satisfies the declared observable contract, then check the visible case before considering the hidden boundary.

feedback.correct: The locked authoring verifier observed the declared bounded facet in both visible and hidden cases.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic authoring check failed; inspect the exact artifact, interaction state, viewport, and observable result without assuming hidden-case details.

criteria.description: Passes the visible and hidden deterministic checks for the declared implementation facet in its pinned authoring environment.

privateAuthorNotes: This code item and its oracle are AI-assisted draft material with reviewer=null and examEligibility=false.
Browser-verifier items are authoring evidence only and must never be routed to the official untrusted-code runner or formal exam pipeline.
items.starterCode: /* TODO: implement the declared bounded CSS facet. */

answer.referenceSolution: @layer reset, base, components; @layer base { :root { color: rgb(20 30 40); } body { color: inherit; } } @layer components { .panel { color: rgb(10 20 30); } main .panel { color: rgb(30 40 50); } }
answer.explanation: The reference is a bounded css artifact for specificity then source order. Passing proves only the declared observable facet, not holistic mastery or human accessibility approval.
